title: Regime-Conditioned Kelly Multiplier Table — Unified Regime Risk Overlay
summary: Consolidate the HMM state, VIX term-structure flag, and breadth gate into a single RegimeContext object that emits a composite risk multiplier (0.0–1.0) applied to the Kelly fraction before each position is sized. This is the integration layer that makes the three regime signals above operate as a unified system rather than independent checks. A 2025 arXiv paper (arXiv:2508.16598 'Sizing the Risk: Kelly, VIX, and Hybrid Approaches') empirically validated that hybrid Kelly+VIX sizing outperforms pure Kelly across multiple regime environments.
expected_impact: A hybrid Kelly+VIX sizing strategy demonstrated annualised returns of 14–23% with materially lower volatility and drawdowns than buy-and-hold in 2024 out-of-sample testing (arXiv 2508.16598); combining regime signal with Kelly reduces tail risk without sacrificing expected return in favourable regimes
